title: GetResponse vs Omnisend (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare GetResponse vs Omnisend. See pricing, features, ratings & find which email marketing platform suits your business best.
intro: GetResponse and Omnisend are both freemium email marketing platforms, but they serve different audiences. GetResponse targets online course creators and webinar hosts with broad marketing tools, while Omnisend focuses on e-commerce brands seeking integrated email and SMS solutions. This guide compares their features, pricing, and performance to help you choose the right platform.

comparison_table.tool_a: Email, webinars, landing pages, courses, automation
comparison_table.tool_b: Email, SMS, push notifications, omnichannel automation

choose_a_if: You're an online course creator or webinar host needing landing pages and funnel-building tools
You want a larger free tier with support for up to 500 subscribers
You prefer a platform with a broader range of marketing features beyond email
choose_b_if: You're an e-commerce business wanting email and SMS marketing in one platform
You need omnichannel automation with push notifications and advanced segmentation
You prioritize customer satisfaction, as indicated by Omnisend's higher Trustpilot rating (4.6 vs 3.7)

faq.a: Neither is universally better—it depends on your use case. GetResponse excels for content creators and offers more diverse marketing features, while Omnisend is superior for e-commerce businesses needing SMS and omnichannel capabilities. Omnisend also has higher customer satisfaction ratings across both G2 and Trustpilot.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, GetResponse or Omnisend?
faq.a: Omnisend has a slightly lower starting price at $16/month compared to GetResponse's $19/month. However, GetResponse offers a larger free tier with 500 subscribers versus Omnisend's 250, making it better for budget-conscious beginners.

faq.q: Can I switch from Omnisend to GetResponse?
faq.a: Yes, most email marketing platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and campaign data. Both tools support standard export formats, though you may need to manually recreate automation workflows. Check with each platform's support for migration assistance during the transfer.
